snippet.title: Ford technician gives CEO Jim Farley ideas to upgrade Ford Mobile Service van
snippet.description: For Ford's Mobile Service technicians, their van is their office. That’s why it’s important to keep improving the vehicle’s tools and features so they can do the best job possible when they’re on the road servicing Ford's retail and Ford Pro commercial customers. As part of his time on the road meeting with customers, employees and dealers, Jim met with Alex at Galpin Ford in California to hear his direct feedback on what’s working and what could be improved to make the van a nicer mobile workplace.

snippet.title: Ford CEO Jim Farley on Ford Mustang GTD, Adopting Tesla's Charging Standard, & Splitting the Company
snippet.description:
MotorTrend's Ed Loh & Jonny Lieberman chat with Ford CEO Jim Farley! The guys discuss the all-new Ford Mustang GTD supercar, Ford adopting Tesla's Charging Standard, the decision behind splitting into "Ford Blue" & "Ford Model e", making EVs vs ICE vehicles, what he learned on his road trip in a Ford Lightning, battery production, the future of SDVs, autonomy with BlueCruise, the EV price war with China, and more! Get the full story HERE ➡️https://bit.ly/3RkrkKw

 #TheInEVitable #Ford #EV #MotorTrend

snippet.title: Ford CEO Jim Farley says the Cybertruck isn’t for “real people who do real work”
snippet.description:
...Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/ford-ceo-jim-farley-says-the-cybertruck-isnt-for-real-people-who-do-real-work/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=ford-ceo-jim-farley-says-the-cybertruck-isnt-for-real-people-who-do-real-work Tesla Drive Canada is reporting that Tesla is preparing to start Cybertruck deliveries later this year, with over 1.5 million reservations. However, Ford CEO Jim Farley doesn't seem concerned about the competition, as he believes that Ford knows truck customers better than anyone and that the Cybertruck is designed for Silicon Valley people, not real people who do real work. Farley made these comments during an interview with CNBC, discussing Ford's deal with Tesla to adopt their North American Charging Standard (NACS) and gain access to the Supercharger network.

While Ford has a strong presence in the pickup market, with decades of experience building the F-150 and holding the title of best-selling truck in the US for nearly half a century, dismissing the Cybertruck's potential impact might be premature. The Cybertruck's unique design has already attracted over 1.5 million reservations, with many first-time truck buyers expressing interest. It remains to be seen how the Cybertruck will perform in the market compared to competitors like Ford's F-150 Lightning. You are listening to YouOn News.

snippet.title: The Man Behind The Best-Selling Truck In 50 Years - Ford CEO Jim Farley | The Fully Charged Podcast
snippet.description:
On the podcast today is first-time guest and Ford CEO, Jim Farley. Prior to joining Ford in 2007, Farley was Group Vice President and General Manager of Lexus, and the Group Vice President responsible for all Toyota Division market planning, advertising and merchandising

Under Farley’s leadership, Ford has boosted investment in electric vehicles to more than $50 billion and has set ambitious targets for scaling their production to 600,000 EVs a year by the end of 2023 and more than 2 million by the end of 2026. Recorded over Zoom at the FC North show in Harrogate, Robert and Jim pull the curtain back and talk EV software, electric transits, the F-150,  battery plants, and the Prius.

snippet.title: Why is Ford coming back to Formula 1? Ford CEO Jim Farley explains the deal with Red Bull Racing
snippet.description:
Ford is coming back to F1. Starting with season 2026, Ford will provide hybrid powertrains to Red Bull Racing, the successful Formula 1 team managed by Christian Horner that last year won the drivers championship with Max Verstappen and the constructors championship too with the help of Mexican driver Sergio "Checo" Pérez.

Why is Ford coming back to Formula 1 now, after almost 20 years out? Jim Farley talked to Siempre Auto during the reveal of the new RB19, the Red Bull car for the 2023 season, an event in which the Ford-Red Bull Racing deal was also announced. 

#SiempreAuto #ford #formula1 #redbullracing 

Siempre Auto es el canal de YouTube en español dedicado a la información del mundo de los carros con enfoque en el mercado de Estados Unidos. Buscamos ayudar a nuestros lectores a tomar las mejores decisiones cuando tengan que comprar, vender o cuidar un auto.

Somos un grupo de expertos y periodistas profesionales que informamos y damos consejos a través de una cobertura transparente, fuentes fiables y un lenguaje accesible.

snippet.title: Ford CEO Jim Farley On Electrification and Tennessee's BlueOval City
